Potion of Healing: This potion restores up to 5 lost points of Health to the figure that drinks it. It may not take a figure above its normal starting Health.
Potion of Strength: A figure that drinks this potion receives +1 Fight for the rest of the game.
Potion of Toughness: The figure that drinks this potion receives +1 Armour for the rest of the game.
Elixir of Speed: The figure who drinks this potion receives +2 Move for the rest of the game. This may not take a figure's Move above 9.
Potion of Invisibility: A figure that drinks this potion is treated as though an Invisibility spell was cast upon it, becoming unable to be targeted or engaged in combat unless it acts (such as attacking, casting, or picking up treasure), which cancels the effect.
Explosive Cocktail: Throwing this cocktail follows all the rules of casting the Grenade spell (a +3 magic attack against everyone within 1.5" of the target point within 14"), except it can be thrown by a non-spellcaster with no Casting Roll needed, the target point must be within 8", and using it cannot replace a move action.
Potion of Teleportation: A figure that drinks this potion is treated as though it had just cast the Teleport spell, immediately moving to any location within line of sight but taking no other actions that turn.
Potion of Elemental Absorption: A figure that drinks this potion ignores the next 5 points of elemental damage it takes.
Cordial of Clearsight: A figure that drinks this potion ignores the effects of Beauty and Invisibility spells for the rest of the game. Furthermore, if this figure moves into combat with an Illusionary Soldier, that Illusionary Soldier is immediately removed from the table.
Poison: This sticky poison coats any weapon except a staff, bow, or crossbow (or a single arrow or crossbow bolt). The next time the weapon causes damage, the figure struck is poisoned unless immune, after which the weapon loses its poisoning ability. This effect is not magic and is unaffected by anything that cancels magic.
Philtre of Fury: The figure that drinks this potion gains +1 Fight and an additional +1 damage modifier on any successful hand-to-hand attack. However, it must, if possible, use all of its actions every turn to move into combat with and fight the closest enemy figure or uncontrolled creature in line of sight that is not already in combat.
Potion of Iron Mind: A figure that drinks this potion gains +5 Will for the rest of the game.
Bottle of Burrowing: A figure that drinks this potion may use any remaining actions in its activation to move directly through terrain, so long as it has enough movement to pass completely through the terrain piece (it may not end its activation inside terrain).
Philtre of Fairy Dust: If this dust is sprinkled over a weapon, that weapon counts as a magic weapon for the rest of the game. It may instead be sprinkled over a single arrow or crossbow bolt, which becomes a one-use magic missile.
Construct Oil: After any game, a wizard may use this oil on one construct in their warband to give it a permanent +1 to its Move stat. Each construct may only ever benefit from construct oil once.
Potion of Fire Breath: A figure that drinks this potion and still has an action remaining in the same activation may use that action to make a +3 elemental magic shooting attack against a figure within 6".
Potion of Preservation: This potion may be used on any figure, other than a wizard, that died in the previous game, perfectly preserving the body for the next five games. During that time an Elixir of Life or the Miraculous Cure spell may be used to bring the figure back to life; a preserved figure does not count toward warband size until revived, and a wizard cannot hire another apprentice while one is preserved. If the figure is not revived within five games, it is permanently dead and removed from the Wizard Sheet.
Elixir of the Chameleon: This potion lets a figure blend in with its surroundings: no other figure may draw line of sight to it from more than 12" away, so it can only be targeted by an attack or spell from within 12".
Cordial of Empowerment: The next spell a spellcaster casts after drinking this potion gets +4 to its Casting Roll but the caster suffers 2 damage, in addition to any other effects of the spell. A spellcaster may only use one Cordial of Empowerment per game, and it cannot be used to cast Out of Game spells.
Shrinking Potion: A figure carrying treasure may use this potion to shrink it down to pocket size, removing any Move, Fight, or swimming penalties for carrying it and allowing the figure to carry a second treasure token as well. If the effect is cancelled while carrying two treasures, one must be dropped immediately, and a dropped shrunk treasure returns to its normal size.
Potion of Restoration: A figure that drinks this potion is immediately restored to its starting Health and cured of any poison or temporary stat reductions. It may also be used after a game to cure the figure of any permanent injuries.
Bottle of Dreams and Nightmares: Usable only by a wizard immediately before a game, this potion requires an immediate Will Roll (TN12). Success grants the wizard an extra 50 experience points after the game (not counting toward the normal maximum); failure deducts 30 experience points from those earned (not below 0), also reducing the maximum obtainable that game to 270.
Shatterstar Draught: If a figure drinks this potion and casts a spell during the same activation, it may attempt to cast that spell twice in a row, declaring a separate target for each attempt before any rolls are made.
Bottle of Darkness: If a figure shatters this bottle on the ground, an unnatural darkness falls upon the battlefield, reducing everyone's line of sight to 12" for the rest of the game.
Ethereal Vacuum: When a character spends an action to open this bottle, all creatures with the Ethereal trait within 8" must make a Will Roll (TN20) or be sucked into the bottle and imprisoned; the figure is removed from the table and any experience for killing it is awarded as normal.
Potion of Invulnerability: A figure that drinks this potion becomes immune to damage from non-magic weapons. Whenever the figure activates, roll a die; on a 17 or higher, the potion's effect ends immediately.
Bottle of Null: If a figure opens this bottle, all spells currently in play are immediately cancelled (this cannot unsummon creatures but does cancel Control spells, including the Control Demon effect from Summon Demon). All spellcasters must also make a Will Roll (TN14) or suffer 1 point of damage.
Elixir of Life: The rarest and most valuable of all potions, it can never be bought and is rarely sold. Used immediately after any game, it brings one figure who died during that game back to life with no ill effects, allowing it to take part in the next game.
